entities.name: Meridian Grace
entities.summary: A container ship that arrived two hours late at Bellingham Harbor on Tuesday morning.

attributes.issue: engine trouble off Point Roberts, fuel pump seized

entities.name: Bellingham Harbor
entities.summary: A harbor with a municipal dock where the Meridian Grace arrived.

entities.name: Captain Voss
entities.summary: The captain of the Meridian Grace who radioed ahead and reported engine trouble.

entities.name: Lloyd's Warehouse
entities.summary: A warehouse where three forklifts and their operators waited for the ship's arrival.

entities.name: Pete Carmichael
entities.summary: A forklift operator at Lloyd's Warehouse.

entities.name: Derek
entities.summary: Pete Carmichael's nephew and a forklift operator at Lloyd's Warehouse.

entities.name: seasonal hire from Tacoma
entities.summary: A seasonal forklift operator at Lloyd's Warehouse from Tacoma.

entities.name: frozen salmon
entities.summary: Forty-two pallets of frozen salmon carried by the Meridian Grace.

entities.name: Pacific Northwest Distributors
entities.summary: A distributor in Seattle that was the destination for the frozen salmon.

entities.name: kelp meal fertilizer
entities.summary: Eight tons of kelp meal fertilizer carried by the Meridian Grace.

entities.name: Skagit Valley farms
entities.summary: Farms in Skagit Valley that were the destination for the kelp meal fertilizer.

entities.name: Jim Reilly
entities.summary: The harbor master who watched the ship's arrival and filled out Coast Guard inspection forms.

entities.name: Dockside Café
entities.summary: A café located below the harbor master's office.

entities.name: Maria Ochoa
entities.summary: Jim Reilly's assistant who updated the arrival log.

entities.name: the ancient Dell desktop
entities.summary: An old Dell desktop computer used to update the arrival log.

entities.name: the manifest
entities.summary: A shipping manifest stamped in Vancouver and handed to Jim Reilly by Captain Voss.

entities.name: Point Roberts
entities.summary: A location off which the Meridian Grace experienced engine trouble.

entities.name: the refrigerated truck
entities.summary: A refrigerated truck from Cascade Cold Storage used to transport the salmon.

entities.name: Cascade Cold Storage
entities.summary: A company that provided the refrigerated truck for transporting salmon.

entities.name: Beth Kowalski
entities.summary: The driver of the refrigerated truck who signed the delivery receipt.

entities.name: Marine Drive
entities.summary: A road that Beth Kowalski drove onto after loading the salmon.

entities.name: Highway 5
entities.summary: A highway that Beth Kowalski took southbound after leaving Marine Drive.

entities.name: Vancouver
entities.summary: The location where the manifest was stamped.

entities.name: Seattle
entities.summary: The location of Pacific Northwest Distributors, destination for the frozen salmon.

entities.name: Everett
entities.summary: The destination of the Meridian Grace after departing Bellingham Harbor.

entities.name: Tacoma
entities.summary: The origin of the seasonal hire forklift operator.

entities.name: three fishing boats
entities.summary: Fishing boats present in the harbor after the Meridian Grace departed.

entities.name: Coast Guard cutter
entities.summary: A Coast Guard cutter undergoing maintenance in the harbor.

entities.name: the creosote pilings
entities.summary: Pilings that have supported the dock since 1947.

extended.name: location

label.description: Human-readable place name when precise coordinates are not available

extended.rationale: Location component should support both precise coordinates and named places; many real-world locations are referenced by name rather than lat/lon

invented.name: vessel
invented.label: Vessel
invented.content: Characteristics specific to ships, boats, and watercraft.

vessel_type.description: Type of vessel (e.g., container ship, tanker, yacht)

propulsion_mode.description: Current propulsion mode (e.g., main engine, auxiliary, sail)

invented.rationale: No existing component captures vessel-specific attributes; these are orthogonal to party (ownership) and meta (general description)

invented.name: incident
invented.label: Incident
invented.content: Problems, issues, or disruptions affecting an entity.

issue.description: Description of the problem or incident

delay.description: Duration of delay caused by the incident

invented.rationale: Incidents are a distinct concern from events (scheduled occurrences) and meta (static description); they represent disruptions that can affect any entity

invented.name: refuel
invented.label: Refueling
invented.content: Details about refueling or resupply operations.

fuel_type.description: Type of fuel (e.g., diesel, gasoline, jet fuel)

invented.rationale: Refueling is a specific operational activity distinct from general events; it has unique attributes (fuel type, pricing) not covered by existing components

invented.name: schedule
invented.label: Schedule
invented.content: Planned timing and routing information for journeys or operations.
invented.rationale: Schedule differs from event component (which is about when something exists/occurs); schedule is about planned movements and routing, applicable to vehicles, shipments, and operations

invented.name: facility
invented.label: Facility
invented.content: Physical infrastructure and features of a place or building.

features.description: List of notable features or infrastructure elements

invented.rationale: Facility characteristics are distinct from location (where) and meta (what); they describe the physical capabilities and infrastructure of a place

invented.name: role
invented.label: Role
invented.content: A person's job, position, or function within a context.

context.description: Organization, project, or context where the role is performed

invented.rationale: Role is orthogonal to party (who someone is) and relation (how entities connect); it describes what someone does in a specific context, reusable across employment, volunteer work, etc.

invented.name: origin
invented.label: Origin
invented.content: Where something or someone comes from.
invented.rationale: Origin is distinct from current location; it represents provenance or source, applicable to people, goods, and other entities

invented.name: cargo
invented.label: Cargo

unit.description: Unit of measurement (e.g., pallets, tons, containers)

state.description: Physical state or condition (e.g., frozen, refrigerated, dry)

destination.description: Where the cargo is being sent

invented.rationale: Cargo has unique attributes (quantity, units, state, destination) not covered by existing components; it's a reusable concept for any transported goods

invented.name: role
invented.label: Role
invented.content: An occupational or functional role that a party performs, including job title and employer.

title.description: The job title or role designation

employer.description: The organization or entity for whom this role is performed

invented.rationale: Role information (harbor master, assistant, truck driver) is distinct from party identity and not covered by existing components. It represents a functional relationship rather than intrinsic party attributes.

invented.name: artifact
invented.label: Artifact
invented.content: Physical objects and their characteristics, including type and condition.

kind.description: The type or category of the physical object

condition.description: The state or condition of the object

invented.rationale: Physical objects (computers, trucks, documents) need representation but don't fit party, post, or other existing components. This captures the essence of tangible items.

invented.name: ownership
invented.label: Ownership
invented.content: Information about who owns or controls an entity.

owner.description: The party that owns or controls this entity

invented.rationale: Ownership is a distinct concept not covered by relation (which is for explicit relationships) or party. It represents a property right that can apply to various entity types.

invented.name: provenance
invented.label: Provenance
invented.content: The origin, history, or source of an entity.

origin.description: Where the entity came from or was created

invented.rationale: Provenance (where something was stamped, created, or originated) is distinct from location (where it currently is) and not covered by existing components.

invented.name: route
invented.label: Route
invented.content: Transportation pathways and directional travel information.

kind.description: Type of route (road, highway, trail, etc.)

direction.description: Direction of travel (north, south, east, west, etc.)

invented.rationale: Roads and highways are distinct from geographic locations; they represent pathways with directional attributes that don't fit location (point-based coordinates) or any other existing component.

invented.name: physical
invented.label: Physical
invented.content: Physical properties and state of tangible objects.

kind.description: Type or category of physical object

invented.rationale: Physical objects (boats, pilings, equipment) need to capture material properties, quantities, and operational status—attributes that are orthogonal to metadata, location, or temporal aspects.
